---
title: D. Configure
---

Set up your environment file, lay out the data directories, and rotate the
`config.yaml` salt before you start the pipeline for the first time.

::: {.callout-important}
## Requires: Install package (step C)
Step C clones the repository and pulls the container images. Complete
[C. Install package](install-package.qmd) before continuing here.
:::

## 1. Copy the environment file

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

## 2. Lay out the data directories

`make mount ROOT=<path>` creates the six host directories the pipeline
bind-mounts into its containers, then prints ready-to-paste `.env` lines so you
do not have to type the paths by hand.

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows: use Git Bash
All `make` commands must be run in **Git Bash**, not PowerShell or cmd.exe.
:::

Replace `./data` with the path where you want pipeline data to live:

```bash
make mount ROOT=./data
```

The command prints something like:

```
# Paste into .env (paths under ./data):
PIPELINE_STORAGE=./data/storage
PIPELINE_OUTBOX_DIR=./data/storage/storage
PIPELINE_REFDATA_DIR=./data/refdata
PIPELINE_IMAGE_ROOT=./data/images
PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_CREDS_DIR=./data/certs
PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_STATE=./data/submitter-state
PIPELINE_SSL_DIR=./data/certs
```

It does **not** write to `.env` â€” copy and paste the output yourself.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
ls ./data
```

Expected: `certs  images  refdata  storage  submitter-state` listed under `./data`.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Skipping `make mount`
If your directories already exist (NAS share, previous install, custom layout),
skip this step and set the path variables in `.env` manually in the next section.
:::

## 3. Fill in `.env`

Open `.env` in any text editor.

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows: save with LF line endings
Docker reads `.env` inside a Linux container. Save with **LF** (Unix) line
endings, not CRLF. VS Code does this automatically; other editors may need a
setting change.
:::

### Must set

These variables have no working defaults â€” the pipeline will not start without
them:

| Variable | What to set | How |
|---|---|---|
| `SLIDETAP_SECRET_KEY` | Random string, â‰Ą 32 characters | Run `openssl rand -hex 32` and paste the output |
| `XLSX_MAPPER_GROUP_NAME` | Your institution's short name | e.g. `karolinska` â€” used as the mapper group label |
| `PIPELINE_STORAGE` | Host path for pipeline storage | From `make mount` output, or set manually |
| `PIPELINE_OUTBOX_DIR` | Must be `<PIPELINE_STORAGE>/storage` | From `make mount` output, or append `/storage` to your storage path |
| `PIPELINE_REFDATA_DIR` | Host path for reference data | From `make mount` output, or set manually |
| `PIPELINE_IMAGE_ROOT` | Host path for whole-slide images | From `make mount` output, or set manually |
| `PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_STATE` | Host path for submitter state | From `make mount` output, or set manually |

To generate a value for `SLIDETAP_SECRET_KEY`:

```bash
openssl rand -hex 32
```

Paste the printed string as the value in `.env`.

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows: forward-slash paths only
Docker's bind-mount parser does not accept Windows backslashes. Write
`./data/storage`, not `.\data\storage` or `C:\data\storage`. If you need an
absolute path, use `C:/data/storage` (forward slashes).
:::

### May change

| Variable | Default | When to change |
|---|---|---|
| `PIPELINE_SERVERNAME` | `localhost` | Set to your host's DNS name or IP if other machines need to reach it |
| `PIPELINE_PORT` | `443` | Change if port 443 is already in use on the host |
| `PIPELINE_SSL_DIR` | `./certs` | Where your TLS certificate and key live |
| `PIPELINE_SSL_CERT` | `server.crt` | TLS certificate filename (inside `PIPELINE_SSL_DIR`) |
| `PIPELINE_SSL_KEY` | `server.key` | TLS private key filename (inside `PIPELINE_SSL_DIR`) |
| `PIPELINE_CREDENTIALS_FILE` | `./credentials.json` | Path to the hashed credentials file (generated in step E) |
| `PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_CREDS_DIR` | `./certs` | Directory holding BigPicture-issued S3 and Crypt4GH keys |
| `PIPELINE_CONFIG_FILE` | `./config.yaml` | Path to the app config file â€” set up in the next step |

### Leave as-is

Everything in the **Dev / advanced** section of `.env` (`PIPELINE_DB_*`,
`XLSX_MAPPER_GROUP_UID`, `SLIDETAP_APP_CREATOR`, `PIPELINE_IMAGE_PREFIX`, etc.)
has working defaults. Do not change these unless you have a specific reason.

## 4. Set up `config.yaml`

Copy the example file:

```bash
cp config.yaml.example config.yaml
```

Open `config.yaml` and replace the `salt` value with a unique secret of **at
least 32 characters**:

```yaml
json_file_auth:
  credentials_file: /credentials.json
  salt: "replace-this-with-your-own-secret-at-least-32-characters"
```

::: {.callout-important}
## Set the salt once â€” never change it afterwards
The salt is mixed into every hashed password. Changing it after you run
`make gen-credentials` (step E) invalidates all existing passwords and locks
everyone out.
:::

### Other settings (optional)

The rest of `config.yaml` controls application behaviour and DICOM conversion.
The defaults are fine for most deployments; adjust only if you have a specific need.

| Setting | Default | When to change |
|---|---|---|
| `restore_projects` | `false` | Set to `true` to let operators resume in-progress submissions after a container restart |
| `dicomization.threads` | `1` | Increase for faster conversion on multi-core hosts (no effect on `.vms` slides) |
| `dicomization.levels` | `all` | Restrict (e.g. `[0, 1, 2]`) for smaller output; fewer levels reduce zoom range in viewers |
| `dicomization.include_labels` | `false` | Set to `true` to include the barcode/cassette label image |
| `dicomization.include_overviews` | `false` | Set to `true` to include the macro overview thumbnail |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

Confirm the salt has been changed:

```bash
grep salt config.yaml
```

The output should show your string, not `change-this-salt-to-something-secret-in-production!!`.
:::

## If something goes wrong

**`make doctor` reports a missing or placeholder variable**

Run `make doctor` to see exactly which variable failed â€” it names the variable
and describes the problem. Return to the relevant section above, set the value,
and run `make doctor` again.

**`make doctor` reports "PIPELINE_CONFIG_FILE still matches config.yaml.example"**

You copied `config.yaml.example` to `config.yaml` but did not change the salt.
Open `config.yaml`, replace the `salt:` value with your own 32+ character
string, save the file, and run `make doctor` again.

**Container bind-mount fails after `docker compose up` (Windows)**

You have backslash paths in `.env`. Docker's bind-mount parser requires forward
slashes on Windows. Open `.env`, replace all `\` with `/`, save with LF line
endings, and restart.
